# checks.imports.ImportsCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.imports.ImportsCheck }

```python
checks.imports.ImportsCheck()
```

Check that all imports in the package can be resolved.

Scans all Python files in the package and attempts to resolve
each import statement. Reports any imports that cannot be found,
which may indicate missing dependencies.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The check identifier ("imports").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of this check.

## Note {.doc-section .doc-section-note}

This check excludes standard library modules and local package
imports from validation. It only flags third-party imports that
cannot be resolved in the current environment.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run the imports check:

```python
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> check = ImportsCheck()
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), {"enabled": True})
>>> result.name
'imports'
```

The check reports unresolvable imports:

```python
>>> # If a file imports 'nonexistent_package'
>>> # result.status == CheckStatus.WARNING
>>> # "Cannot import 'nonexistent_package'" in result.details
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.imports.ImportsCheck.run) | Check imports in all Python files. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.imports.ImportsCheck.run }

```python
checks.imports.ImportsCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Check imports in all Python files.

Parses each Python file to extract import statements, then
attempts to import each module to verify it exists.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary for this check. Currently no check-specific options are used.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - OK status if all imports can be resolved

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - NOTE status if no Python files found

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - WARNING status if some imports cannot be resolved

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - ERROR status if Python files have syntax errors
